title: Fourth Year
prose: The workload steps up again this year — homework that actually eats into a whole evening, professors who've stopped bothering to explain why something matters and just expect you to keep up.

Even so, the Great Hall on the first night feels exactly as it always has: too loud, too bright, and — for one unrepeatable minute, while the first-years file in behind Professor McGonagall — exactly as overwhelming as it must be for them.

choices.label: Catch up with housemates over the feast.
